title: Workday (WDAY) Stock Jumps 11% on Q3 Earnings Beat
text: Although the cloud-computing segment represented one of the hardest hit within the broad technology ecosystem, financial and human-capital management software provider Workday (NASDAQ: WDAY ) cut through the muck, surging 11% in Wednesday's morning session. Providing the boost for WDAY stock was a strong earnings performance that beat expectations for the top and bottom lines.

title: Why Workday Stock Was Climbing Today
text: A better-than-expected earnings report gave the software-as-a-service specialist a boost.

title: Upbeat Quarterly Results Boost Workday Stock
text: Workday Inc (NASDAQ:WDAY) is up 11.5% to trade at $159.78 this morning after the software name shared better-than-expected third-quarter earnings and revenue.

title: Nasdaq SaaS Stocks Are Making Big Moves. Here's Why.
text: The latest financial reports from two key players offer different looks at the software industry.

title: Workday (WDAY) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Workday (WDAY) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 17.86% and 1.18%, respectively, for the quarter ended October 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Workday Stock Climbs As Earnings Manage To Top Wall Street Views
text: Workday reported earnings that fell from a year earlier but topped estimates. The company slightly raised its full-year subscription outlook.

title: Workday stock rises 5% following hiked outlook, share buybacks announced
text: Workday Inc. shares rallied in the extended session Tuesday after the human-resources cloud-software company hiked its outlook and launched a share buyback program.

title: Workday Has Fallen 50% And I'm Buying
text: Following Friday's SAAS sell-off, Workday, the leading SAAS ERP company has fallen over 50% in 2022, despite a strong operational performance. With a broad product offering and customer base, Workday has a long runway for double digit revenue growth.
